stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 3 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 8 observations with mean 80.9 points (within-school SD = 7.7). school B has 20 observations with mean 75.1 points (within-school SD = 5.9). school C has 8 observations with mean 77.4 points (within-school SD = 8.2). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.0 and 12.5 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school C: 77.3 points (shrunk from raw 77.4). Range [77.3, 77.4] as between-school SD varies from 5.0 to 12.5. Pooled mean: 76.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 76.9 points (weighted average of 3 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=8.3: B = 0.891.
Interval [77.3, 77.4] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (77.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (76.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
